preamble: The spirantization or fronting of a voiceless uvular stop to a voiceless velar fricative, common in Turkic and Arabic regional variants.

examples.note: In many dialects, the uvular stop /q/ is realized as a velar or uvular fricative.
Spirantization of /q/ is observed in certain rural or Bedouin dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
